diagrams.title: Pascal's Triangle Construction
diagrams.description: Triangular array showing binomial coefficients C(n,r) for n=0 to 6
elements: Each element equals sum of two elements above
diagrams.purpose: Visualize binomial coefficients and their relationships

diagrams.title: Permutation vs Combination Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows difference between ordered arrangements and unordered selections using 3 objects A,B,C
elements: PERMUTATIONS (order matters): ABC, ACB, BAC, BCA, CAB, CBA = 3! = 6
Choosing 2 from 3: Permutations: AB, BA, AC, CA, BC, CB = 6 ways; Combinations: {A,B}, {A,C}, {B,C} = 3 ways
diagrams.purpose: Understand when to use permutation vs combination and the relationship P(n,r) = C(n,r)×r!

diagrams.title: Circular Permutation Fixing Method
diagrams.description: Shows why we divide by n to get (n-1)! for circular arrangements
elements: Linear arrangement of 4: ABCD, BCDA, CDAB, DABC are all DIFFERENT (4! = 24)
Circular arrangement: These 4 are the SAME (rotations)
Fix one position to eliminate rotation symmetry: remaining 3 positions = 3! = 6
For n objects: (n-1)! unique circular arrangements
Note: For necklace/flip symmetry, further divide by 2
diagrams.purpose: Understand why circular permutations use (n-1)! formula
